title: Antiparasitic Drugs
summary: الادوية المضادة للطفيليات تشمل ثلاث مجموعات: ادوية الاوليات (Protozoa) مثل Metronidazole للـ Giardia والـ Entamoeba وادوية الملاريا، وادوية الديدان الطفيلية (Helminths) مثل Albendazole وIvermectin وPraziquantel، وادوية الطفيليات الخارجية (Ectoparasites) مثل Permethrin. Metronidazole دواء شامل للبكتيريا اللاهوائية والطفيليات الاولية ويسبب تفاعل مشابه للـ Disulfiram مع الكحول. Albendazole يعمل بتثبيط تكوين الانابيب الدقيقة مما يحرم الطفيل من الجلوكوز ويؤدي الى موته.

key_drugs.name: Metronidazole
key_drugs.note: Prodrug activated in anaerobes; treats Giardia, Entamoeba, anaerobes; disulfiram-like with alcohol

key_drugs.name: Artemisinin (Artemether)
key_drugs.note: Free radical formation via iron activation; first-line for P. falciparum

key_drugs.name: Chloroquine
key_drugs.note: Inhibits heme detoxification; resistance common in P. falciparum

key_drugs.name: Pyrimethamine
key_drugs.note: Inhibits folate synthesis; used in prophylaxis; bone marrow suppression

key_drugs.name: Albendazole
key_drugs.note: Inhibits microtubule formation; broad-spectrum for intestinal and tissue worms

key_drugs.name: Mebendazole
key_drugs.note: Same MOA as Albendazole; mainly for intestinal worms

key_drugs.name: Ivermectin
key_drugs.note: Enhances GABA → parasite paralysis; for Strongyloides, Onchocerca, ectoparasites

key_drugs.name: Praziquantel
key_drugs.note: Increases calcium permeability → muscle contraction/paralysis; for Schistosoma, tapeworms

key_drugs.name: Permethrin
key_drugs.note: Disrupts sodium channels → neurotoxicity in parasites; first-line for scabies and lice

mnemonics.title: Metro = طريق بلا هواء
mnemonics.body: Metronidazole = 'Metro' يسير في طريق بلا هواء (anaerobic). في الكائنات اللاهوائية يتحول الى جذور حرة (free radicals) تحطم DNA فتقتلها. الكائنات الهوائية (كخلايانا) لا تفعّله لذا هو آمن نسبيا.

mnemonics.title: GEM - طفيليات الاوليات وعلاجها
mnemonics.body:
G = Giardia → Metronidazole (greasy stool)
E = Entamoeba → Metronidazole + luminal agent (bloody stool)
M = Malaria → Artemisinin-based combination (cyclic fever)

mnemonics.title: Albendazole يجوع الدودة
mnemonics.body: تخيل دودة جائعة (deprived of glucose). Albendazole يكسر انابيبها الدقيقة (microtubules) اللازمة لامتصاص الجلوكوز. بدون طاقة (ATP) تموت الدودة جوعا. الصورة: دودة تشبك يديها على بطنها الخاوي.

mnemonics.title: Praziquantel يسبب تشنج
mnemonics.body: Praziquantel = 'برازي يكانتل' يفتح قنوات الكالسيوم في الدودة (Schistosoma/Tapeworm) فتدخل كميات هائلة من Ca++ تسبب تقلصا عضليا شديدا وشللا ثم موت الطفيل.

mnemonics.title: SNAP - علاج الديدان حسب المكان
mnemonics.body:
S = Stay in gut (intestinal worms) → Albendazole
N = Nematodes → Albendazole/Ivermectin
A = All tissues (Echinococcus liver cysts) → Albendazole
P = Parasites in blood/vessels (Schistosoma) → Praziquantel

flashcards.front: How does Metronidazole work?
flashcards.back: A prodrug activated in anaerobic organisms to form free radicals that damage DNA and cause cell death.

flashcards.front: Why does Metronidazole alone not eradicate luminal Entamoeba cysts?
flashcards.back: Metronidazole does not reliably eliminate luminal (intestinal) cysts, so a luminal agent such as Paromomycin is added.

flashcards.front: What is the dangerous interaction of Metronidazole with alcohol?
flashcards.back: Disulfiram-like reaction: nausea, vomiting, flushing, and chest pain upon alcohol consumption.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Artemisinin in treating malaria?
flashcards.back: Iron present inside the parasite activates Artemisinin to form free radicals that damage parasite proteins and membranes.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Chloroquine in malaria?
flashcards.back: Inhibits conversion of toxic Heme to Hemozoin (non-toxic), causing Heme accumulation that kills the parasite.

flashcards.front: What is the treatment of choice for P. falciparum?
flashcards.back: Artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT).

flashcards.front: How do Albendazole/Mebendazole work?
flashcards.back: They inhibit microtubule formation in the parasite, preventing glucose absorption and depleting energy (ATP), leading to its death.

flashcards.front: What are the clinical indications for Albendazole?
flashcards.back: Enterobiasis (pinworm), Ascariasis, Hookworm, Hydatid disease (Echinococcus) in the liver and lungs.

flashcards.front: How does Ivermectin work?
flashcards.back: Increases permeability of GABA-gated chloride channels, causing paralysis and death of the parasite.

flashcards.front: What are the uses of Ivermectin?
flashcards.back: Strongyloidiasis, Onchocerciasis, severe or treatment-resistant scabies and lice.

flashcards.front: How does Praziquantel work?
flashcards.back: Increases calcium permeability of the parasite membrane, causing muscular contraction, paralysis, and death.

flashcards.front: What is the treatment for Schistosomiasis?
flashcards.back: Praziquantel — because Schistosoma resides in blood vessels and requires a drug that reaches the tissues.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Permethrin?
flashcards.back: Disrupts sodium channels in the parasite, causing neurotoxicity and death.

flashcards.front: What is the first-line treatment for Scabies and Lice?
flashcards.back: Topical Permethrin is first-line. Oral Ivermectin for severe or resistant cases.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between luminal and tissue protozoa and their treatment?
flashcards.back: Luminal (intestinal only): luminal agents. Tissue-invasive (invades tissues): Metronidazole. Some cases require both.

flashcards.front: What are the distinguishing features to differentiate Giardia, Entamoeba, and Malaria?
flashcards.back: Giardia: greasy, foul-smelling stool, no fever. Entamoeba: bloody stool (amoebic dysentery). Malaria: cyclic fever.

flashcards.front: What distinguishes Albendazole pharmacokinetically from Mebendazole?
flashcards.back: Albendazole distributes better into tissues (brain, liver, lungs, muscle). Its absorption is enhanced by a fatty meal.

flashcards.front: How are parasites classified?
flashcards.back: Protozoa (Giardia, Entamoeba, Plasmodium), Helminths, Ectoparasites.

flashcards.front: What determines the choice of antiparasitic drug?
flashcards.back: The type of parasite and the location of infection (luminal vs. tissue).

flashcards.front: What is the antiparasitic use of Metronidazole?
flashcards.back: Amoebiasis, giardiasis, and trichomoniasis (anaerobic protozoa).

flashcards.front: What is the use of Albendazole/Mebendazole?
flashcards.back: Nematodes (roundworms); inhibit polymerization of the worm's microtubules.

flashcards.front: What is the use of Praziquantel?
flashcards.back: Flatworms (cestodes/trematodes) such as Schistosoma.

flashcards.front: What is the use of Ivermectin?
flashcards.back: Onchocerciasis, Ascariasis, scabies, and lice.

flashcards.front: What are the antimalarial drugs?
flashcards.back: Chloroquine, Mefloquine, Primaquine (for hepatic stages), Artemisinins, Quinine.

flashcards.front: What is the use of Permethrin?
flashcards.back: Scabies and lice (ectoparasites — topical).

mcqs.q: A patient presents with bloody stool and is diagnosed with invasive Entamoeba histolytica. What is the optimal treatment?
mcqs.explanation: Tissue amoebiasis requires Metronidazole for the tissue stage and a luminal agent such as Paromomycin to eliminate luminal cysts.

mcqs.q: What is the characteristic side effect of Metronidazole when alcohol is consumed?
mcqs.explanation: Metronidazole causes a disulfiram-like reaction with alcohol manifested by nausea, vomiting, flushing, and tachycardia.

mcqs.q: A traveler returning from Africa presents with cyclic fever. Malaria P. falciparum is diagnosed. What is the first-line treatment?
mcqs.explanation: ACT (artemisinin-based combination therapy) is the first-line treatment for P. falciparum due to widespread Chloroquine resistance.

mcqs.q: What is the mechanism of action of Albendazole?
options: Inhibits microtubule formation preventing glucose absorption
Forms free radicals that damage DNA
mcqs.explanation: Albendazole inhibits microtubule formation in the parasite, preventing glucose absorption and depleting its energy.

mcqs.q: A child has nocturnal perianal itching. What is the most likely diagnosis?
mcqs.explanation: Nocturnal perianal pruritus is a hallmark of Enterobiasis (pinworm). Treatment: Albendazole.

mcqs.q: A patient has liver cysts diagnosed as Echinococcus (Hydatid disease). What is the treatment?
mcqs.explanation: Albendazole penetrates tissues well (liver, lungs, brain) and is used for Hydatid disease.

mcqs.q: Praziquantel is the drug of choice for which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: Praziquantel is the DOC for Schistosomiasis (which inhabits blood vessels) and for tapeworms.

mcqs.q: What is the first-line treatment for severe resistant scabies?
mcqs.explanation: Topical Permethrin is first-line for ordinary scabies, but severe or resistant cases require oral Ivermectin.

options: Enhances GABA-mediated chloride influx causing parasite paralysis
mcqs.explanation: Ivermectin enhances permeability of GABA-gated chloride channels, causing paralysis and death of the parasite.

mcqs.q: A patient has iron-deficiency anemia with a positive stool examination for worms. What is the most likely diagnosis and its treatment?
mcqs.explanation: Iron-deficiency anemia with worms suggests Hookworm, which sucks blood. Treatment is Albendazole.

mcqs.q: The hepatic stage of malaria is treated with:
